[10/12/2025, 11:56:26 AM] Mauro Zucula: Morning team,

The port-side slimes extraction pump sank during the night.
We are currently preparing to start the pump rescue process.

Below is the step-by-step procedure for the pump rescue:
1. Pump isolations. 
2. Secure the pump with a rope and anchor it to the shore.
3. Expose the pump’s anchoring point and the winch connection, then disconnect them.
4. Pull the pump to a position that allows disconnection of the electrical cables and the delivery hose.
5. Move the pump until it is fully retrieved onto shore.

[20/03/2026, 1:20:35 PM] Monteiro Junior 😇: Hi @⁨~Dago⁩ 

At the TSF, the current level is 23.5 mRL, which is close to the slime level; as such, there is effectively no free water available. The electric decant pumps will continue transporting slimes, so I recommend we retain only the supernatant and PDP Pumps flows.

The main challenge is at the PDP, where the water level remains high. To address this, I propose the following actions:

1. Maximize PDP pump return by operating the fourth pump. 
2. Bring the 2nd slimes extraction line back online. The engineering team has provided the clamp and aligned the installation, with an estimated time of 2 hours. This will allow us to run 2 slimes extraction lines. 
3. The third slimes extraction line is currently offline due to ongoing connection works to integrate it with the fourth slimes extraction line, which is expected to be available on Monday.

The flocculant system is currently down due to a stolen HT cable, which has now been replaced; we are awaiting LT cable connection. This issue is Contributing in quick displacement of slimes at PDP and returning to the pond. The engineering team is on-site and will feedback ET @⁨~Damião Cossa⁩ ATT
